---
title: "final_project"
author: Josephine Andoh
output: html_document
---

```{r}
library(tidyverse)

pgcrime17_23 <- read_csv("PGCRIME17-23.csv")

pgcrime23_26 <- read_csv("PGCRIME23-26.csv")

mococrime <- read_csv("C:/Users/jojoa/Downloads/MOCOCRIME.csv")
```

Notes:

Prince George's County crime data from February 2, 2017, to July 5, 2023, can be found at this link (https://data.princegeorgescountymd.gov/Public-Safety/Crime-Incidents-February-2017-to-5th-July-2023/wb4e-w4nf/about_data). 

The county's crime data from July 1, 2023, to April 2, 2026, can be found at this link (https://data.princegeorgescountymd.gov/Public-Safety/Crime-Incidents-July-2023-to-Present/xjru-idbe/about_data).

Montgomery County's crime data from July 1, 2016, to April 10, 2026, can be found at this link (https://data.montgomerycountymd.gov/Public-Safety/Crime/icn6-v9z3/about_data).

Prince George's County's datasets contain information on 18 crime categories, including accidents, assaults, commercial and residential breaking and entering and commercial and vehicle robbery. The columns that will be the most important for my analysis are date and clearance code inc type. I am missing definitions for the county's crime categorizations - for example, what classifies as a weapon in "assault with a weapon?" Does assault include all types of sexual assault? I am also missing data on homicides, incidents of driving under the influence, drug abuse incidents and financial crimes.

Montgomery County's dataset contains information on over 400 crime categories, including abortion, incest with a minor, drunkenness and robbery with a knife. The columns that will be the most important for my analysis are start date time, crime name 2 and crime name 3. I am missing justifications for the county's crime categorizations - Why are abortion, mental illness and suicides classified as crimes? Why is "liquor" classified as a crime when the specific offense has not been described?

Now that I have looked at the datasets again, I am a bit concerned about my plan to group certain crimes together. If I cannot find definitions for certain crimes, I will  not be sure what category I can put them in. 


My questions:

Why isn't there a centralized system for categorizing crimes across Maryland? Why is Prince George's County allowed to generalize while Montgomery County is extremely specific?

How can I change the date-month-year format so that I can filter the crimes in chronological order?

How can I exclude the dates and years I am not analyzing? Are there filters I can apply?

Should I analyze my data based on days, weeks or months? I want to do months due to the sheer volume of my data. Is that a good idea?

How many years' worth of data should I analyze? In HW9 (Car Thefts Confidence Interval & Error), Mr. Salama said that five years' worth of data was too long of a period to analyze.

Is there any way I can combine the Prince George's County crime datasets together to facilitate my analysis?
